title: Require an SBOM and provenance for shipped dependencies
description: Generate a software bill of materials and capture build provenance so every shipped component has a known source, version, and origin that can be audited.
body: Use when assembling a release whose dependency origins must be auditable. Do not ship a build whose component set is unknown, and avoid trusting a binary with no recorded source. Actions: generate an SBOM (CycloneDX/SPDX) listing each component with version and source, capture build provenance/attestation tying artifacts to the source commit and builder, and inspect the SBOM against the lockfile for completeness; record it alongside the release. Validation signals include an SBOM that matches the resolved dependency tree and a provenance attestation verifiable against the build. Risk boundary: a component with no identifiable source or provenance is an unaudited supply-chain risk; flag it for human review and deny promotion rather than shipping an unknown origin.
